expectations.text: Every function in text_utils.py has a corresponding .py file (9 functions: strip_images, strip_urls, strip_references, strip_acknowledgments, strip_extra_blanks, _split_sections, _classify_headings_with_llm, extract_relevant_sections, preprocess_md)
expectations.evidence: All 9 files present in examples/text_utils/: strip_images.py, strip_urls.py, strip_references.py, strip_acknowledgments.py, strip_extra_blanks.py, _split_sections.py, _classify_headings_with_llm.py, extract_relevant_sections.py, preprocess_md.py

expectations.text: Every generated .py file runs successfully with `python <file>` (exit code 0)
expectations.evidence: All 9 files executed with exit code 0. Each produced correct output demonstrating the function's behavior.

expectations.text: _classify_headings_with_llm.py uses a mock instead of making real API calls, yet still demonstrates the function's behavior
expectations.evidence: The file uses unittest.mock (MagicMock, patch) to mock openai.OpenAI. A _create_mock_response helper builds a fake ChatCompletion response returning JSON indices [1, 4, 5]. The mock is applied via `with patch('openai.OpenAI')` and `patch.dict(os.environ, {...})` so no real API call is made, yet the function's full parsing logic (regex extraction of JSON array, index validation) executes end-to-end.

expectations.text: Sample inputs use realistic scientific paper markdown content with real-looking headings, image tags, URLs, etc.
expectations.evidence: All files use realistic scientific paper content: strip_images.py has carotenoid biosynthesis text with CDN and local image tags; strip_urls.py has GEO/DOI/journal URLs; strip_references.py has OsNAS2 iron biofortification references; _split_sections.py has anthocyanin paper with YAML frontmatter and multiple sections; _classify_headings_with_llm.py uses headings like '# Enrichment of Tomato Fruit with Anthocyanins', '# Introduction', '# Results', etc. All content reflects the domain of plant nutrient metabolism research.

expectations.text: Each file has a docstring explaining the function's purpose
expectations.evidence: Every file has a module-level docstring (triple-quoted string at the top) explaining the function name, what it does, the source location, and how to run it. Additionally, each target function within the file has its own docstring explaining behavior.
